name: get_coins_markets

vs_currency.description:
target currency of coins and market data 
 *refers to [`/simple/supported_vs_currencies`](/reference/simple-supported-currencies).

category.description:
filter based on coins' category 
 *refers to [`/coins/categories/list`](/reference/coins-categories-list).

ids.description:
coins' IDs, comma-separated if querying more than 1 coin. 
 *refers to [`/coins/list`](/reference/coins-list).

include_tokens.description:
for `symbols` lookups, specify `all` to include all matching tokens 
 Default `top` returns top-ranked tokens (by market cap or volume)

names.description: coins' names, comma-separated if querying more than 1 coin.

order.description: sort result by field, default: market_cap_desc

per_page.description:
total results per page, default: 100 
 Valid values: 1...250

precision.description: decimal place for currency price value

price_change_percentage.description:
include price change percentage timeframe, comma-separated if query more than 1 timeframe 
 Valid values: 1h, 24h, 7d, 14d, 30d, 200d, 1y

symbols.description: coins' symbols, comma-separated if querying more than 1 coin.

jq_filter.title: jq Filter
jq_filter.description:
A jq filter to apply to the response to include certain fields. Consult the output schema in the tool description to see the fields that are available.

For example: to include only the `name` field in every object of a results array, you can provide ".results[].name".

For more information, see the [jq documentation](https://jqlang.org/manual/).

name: get_coins_top_gainers_losers

duration.description:
filter result by time range 
 Default value: `24h`

price_change_percentage.description:
include price change percentage timeframe, comma-separated if query more than 1 price change percentage timeframe 
 Valid values: 1h, 24h, 7d, 14d, 30d, 200d, 1y

top_coins.description:
filter result by market cap ranking (top 300 to 1000) or all coins (including coins that do not have market cap) 
 Default value: `1000`

A jq filter to apply to the response to include certain fields. Consult the output schema in the tool description to see the fields that are available.

token.description:
return OHLCV for token 
 use this to invert the chart 
 Available values: 'base', 'quote' or token address 
 Default value: 'base'

aggregate.description:
time period to aggregate each OHLCV 
 Available values (day): `1` 
 Available values (hour): `1` , `4` , `12` 
 Available values (minute): `1` , `5` , `15` 
 Available values (second): `1`, `15`, `30` 
 Default value: 1

before_timestamp.description: return OHLCV data before this timestamp (integer seconds since epoch)

currency.description:
return OHLCV in USD or quote token 
 Default value: usd

limit.description:
number of OHLCV results to return, maximum 1000 
 Default value: 100

name: get_pools_onchain_megafilter
description: This endpoint allows you to **query pools based on various filters across all networks on GeckoTerminal**

buys_duration.description:
duration for buy transactions metric 
 Default value: 24h

checks.description:
filter options for various checks, comma-separated if more than one 
 Available values: `no_honeypot`, `good_gt_score`, `on_coingecko`, `has_social`

dexes.description:
filter pools by DEXes, comma-separated if more than one 
 DEX ID refers to [/networks/{network}/dexes](/reference/dexes-list)

fdv_usd_max.description: maximum fully diluted value in USD

fdv_usd_min.description: minimum fully diluted value in USD

include.description:
attributes to include, comma-separated if more than one to include 
 Available values: `base_token`, `quote_token`, `dex`, `network`

networks.description:
filter pools by networks, comma-separated if more than one 
 Network ID refers to [/networks](/reference/networks-list)

sells_duration.description:
duration for sell transactions metric 
 Default value: 24h

sort.description:
sort the pools by field 
 Default value: h6_trending

tx_count_duration.description:
duration for transaction count metric 
 Default value: 24h

name: get_simple_price
description:
When using this tool, always use the `jq_filter` parameter to reduce the response size and improve performance.

Only omit if you're sure you don't need the data.

This endpoint allows you to **query the prices of one or more coins by using their unique Coin API IDs**

# Response Schema
```json
{
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: true
}
```

vs_currencies.description:
target currency of coins, comma-separated if querying more than 1 currency. 
 *refers to [`/simple/supported_vs_currencies`](/reference/simple-supported-currencies).

ids.description:
coins' IDs, comma-separated if querying more than 1 coin. 
 *refers to [`/coins/list`](/reference/coins-list).

include_tokens.description:
for `symbols` lookups, specify `all` to include all matching tokens 
 Default `top` returns top-ranked tokens (by market cap or volume)

names.description: coins' names, comma-separated if querying more than 1 coin.

precision.description: decimal place for currency price value

symbols.description: coins' symbols, comma-separated if querying more than 1 coin.

jq_filter.title: jq Filter
jq_filter.description:
A jq filter to apply to the response to include certain fields. Consult the output schema in the tool description to see the fields that are available.

For example: to include only the `name` field in every object of a results array, you can provide ".results[].name".

For more information, see the [jq documentation](https://jqlang.org/manual/).

name: get_simple_supported_vs_currencies

jq_filter.title: jq Filter
jq_filter.description:
A jq filter to apply to the response to include certain fields. Consult the output schema in the tool description to see the fields that are available.

For example: to include only the `name` field in every object of a results array, you can provide ".results[].name".

For more information, see the [jq documentation](https://jqlang.org/manual/).

precision.description: decimal place for currency price value

name: search_docs
description:
Search for documentation for how to use the client to interact with the API.
The tool will return an array of Markdown-formatted documentation pages.

language.description: The language for the SDK to search for.
